rationale: Design-fidelity re-verify: all 7 asks landed without inventing or dropping scope, and the one that could creep — D, the instruct-renderer refactor — is the ratchet applied to a REAL existing duplicate. I confirmed PanelStateEntry.verdict() (internal/instruct/panelstate.go:87) is a standalone reproduction of the decision matrix (its own comment: 'mirroring the Bead 4 decision matrix, Spec 093 Reqs 11/12'), reproducing every branch inline and NOT calling panel.PanelGateDecision — exactly the two-places drift ADR-0040 ratchets down. Critically, internal/instruct/** is already claimed by the workflow OWNERSHIP glob, so adding it to Impacted Domains introduces NO new domain: the 'exactly one Impacted-Domain (workflow)' claim R5's parity check keys on is untouched (the diff appended a sentence to the existing workflow bullet, not a new bullet). D also CORRECTS round-1 R2's factually-wrong parenthetical that implied PanelStateEntry 'already wraps' the decision — a fidelity improvement, not scope. The other asks are faithful: B/C are the same round+SHA co-bump invariant extended to the BRIEF header + a fail-safe argument (divergent target → false-BLOCK never false-PASS), no new behavior; A narrows R6 to anchored-link bullets, resolving 110's self-inconsistency (its own ADR-0040/0030 prose mentions are now correctly outside extraction); E/F executablize R4/R7a as ACs; G is my own round-1 placement finding, now reconciled at rebase-forward. Build AC extended to ./internal/instruct/.... My round-1 APPROVE stands, strengthened.

findings.title: Ask D is a scope-neutral fidelity fix, not scope creep — verified against the tree
findings.detail: The concern flagged for round-2 re-check was whether pulling internal/instruct into 110 widens scope. It does not, on two independently-verified grounds. (1) The duplicate is real and pre-existing: PanelStateEntry.verdict() at internal/instruct/panelstate.go:87 hand-reproduces the round-mismatch / reviewed_head_sha-staleness / incompleteness / abandoned branches and returns GateBlock/GatePass/GateWarn directly, referencing pre-109 prose ('/ms-panel-run step 0', 'Spec 093 Reqs 11/12') — it does NOT call panel.PanelGateDecision (its internal/panel import supplies only the PanelGateVerdict type). Refactoring it to delegate is precisely ADR-0040's ratchet on an existing L-drift, the same class as the R8 skill-dedup, so it belongs in this spec. (2) It adds no domain: internal/instruct/** is already within .mindspec/domains/workflow/OWNERSHIP.yaml, so the sole-workflow Impacted-Domains claim (load-bearing for R5's 'extracts exactly one Impacted-Domain') survives; the round-2 diff appended one sentence to the existing workflow bullet rather than adding a bullet, and the plain-prose read-only-domains note is unchanged. As a bonus, D repairs round-1 R2's inaccurate 'or the PanelStateEntry renderer that already wraps them' — which falsely implied instruct already delegated. Net: fidelity improved, boundary held.

findings.title: Round-1 placement finding (dangling ../../adr/ link) now explicitly reconciled — carried forward as observation only
findings.detail: My round-1 finding noted the ## ADR Touchpoints links use ../../adr/, correct for the canonical flat .mindspec/specs/ home but dangling from the current .mindspec/docs/specs/ staging path. Ask G folds this into Open Questions as the 'Placement note': the links are written for the flat destination and the spec file is reconciled to .mindspec/specs/ as part of the same rebase-forward before plan-approve. This is the honest resolution — it neither changes the link form now (which would diverge from every legacy spec's convention) nor lets the discrepancy pass silently, and R6 keys on the ADR ID against adr.Store, not the markdown path, so the link never trips the parity check regardless. No change required at spec-approve.
